message:
test(fsqlite-pager): self-allocated page test also asserts read-your-own-write invariant

Extend the `self_allocated_eof_page_stays_out_of_conflict_surface`
integration test to round-trip a read after the write so the same
transaction reading back the page it just allocated + wrote sees the
correct byte pattern.

- After `txn.write_page(..., page, &[0xA5; 64])`, call
  `txn.get_page(&cx, page)` and assert `.as_ref()[0] == 0xA5`.
- Pins the read-your-own-write contract the pager advertises for
  Concurrent transactions: the allocating txn must be able to see
  its own extension page immediately, regardless of whether the
  page is part of the cross-process conflict surface or not.

Also a tiny rustfmt wrap on the allocate_page call to match the
surrounding block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
